achieve_passing_justification: Initial release. Passing badge submission planned after v0.0.1 tag lands and the CI history has at least one green run.
achieve_silver_justification: Silver artefact criteria (assurance_case, dco enforcement, per-file SPDX headers) are Met from the initial import; achieve_silver stays Unmet until achieve_passing lands first.
assurance_case_justification:
docs/security-model.md is the project's formal assurance case: scope, trust boundaries (5), security claims C1-C5 each with linked source evidence, threats considered vs out of scope, assumptions, and compensating controls for the single-maintainer bus factor.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral-sync/blob/main/docs/security-model.md
bus_factor_justification:
Numerical bus factor is 1 (single maintainer). MAINTAINERS.md catalogues every load-bearing external service and codifies voluntary hand-off, 6-month community-fork threshold, and emergency compromise response. Structural at the Met bar until a co-maintainer joins.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral-sync/blob/main/MAINTAINERS.md
code_of_conduct_justification:
CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md adopts the Contributor Covenant v2.1 with the maintainer's email as the enforcement contact.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral-sync/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
contribution_justification:
CONTRIBUTING.md documents fork/branch/PR flow, required verification (make test/build), signed-commit requirement, DCO sign-off requirement, and coding standards.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral-sync/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md 'Pull Request Checklist' section enumerates acceptance criteria: make test passes, make build succeeds, README updated if behaviour changed, all commits signed and DCO-signed off.
contributors_unassociated_justification: Structural blocker at Gold: all meaningful contributions originate from the single maintainer. The criterion requires two committers from unrelated organisations, which a solo project cannot honestly claim.
copyright_per_file_justification: Every .go source file carries `// SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2026 Sebastien Rousseau <sebastian.rousseau@gmail.com>` at the top.
crypto_certificate_verification_justific: Go stdlib's crypto/tls verifies server certificates by default; corral-sync never sets InsecureSkipVerify: true (grep-verifiable across the codebase).
crypto_published_justification: corral-sync delegates cryptography to git (SSH/HTTPS), Go stdlib TLS, and the sigstore/cosign toolchain. No home-rolled crypto.
crypto_used_network_justification: No server-side network cryptography. Client-side TLS to gitlab.com / gitea instances / ghcr.io uses Go stdlib defaults (TLS 1.3).
dco_justification:
Every commit in every PR must carry a `Signed-off-by:` trailer matching the author. Enforced by .github/workflows/dco.yml (fails the PR on any missing or malformed sign-off). Contributor workflow documented in CONTRIBUTING.md.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral-sync/blob/main/.github/workflows/dco.yml
delivery_mitm_justification: Distribution is via HTTPS: github.com/releases, ghcr.io. No plain-HTTP paths.
delivery_unsigned_justification: All release artifacts have SHA-256 checksums in the auto-generated checksums.txt (goreleaser) + SLSA v1.0 provenance from actions/attest-build-provenance. Docker image is cosign-signed.
dependency_monitoring_justification: GitHub Dependabot alerts + weekly Dependabot PRs configured in .github/dependabot.yml for gomod, github-actions, and docker ecosystems.
description_good_justification: README.md's opening paragraph describes what corral-sync does: 'Mirror a corral-organised local repository tree to GitLab and Gitea with absolute-parity semantics.'
discussion_justification: GitHub Issues is the primary discussion channel — searchable, publicly viewable, no client install required.
documentation_basics_justification: README.md is the primary documentation: quick start, flags & environment, design, deployment link. DEPLOYMENT.md is the operator's guide.
documentation_interface_justification: GoDoc for every exported symbol; internal packages are documented with package-level docs. README's flags table documents every CLI flag and env var.
documentation_security_justification: SECURITY.md documents the private disclosure channel. docs/security-model.md is the formal assurance case.
dynamic_analysis_justification: Add `go test -race ./...` to the Go CI reusable pipeline in a follow-up; currently not enforced.
english_justification: README, CHANGELOG, SECURITY, CONTRIBUTING, all issues, PRs, and code comments are in English.
external_dependencies_justification: Stdlib only for the runtime binary. go.mod has zero non-stdlib requires. Verifiable via `go list -m all`.
floss_license_justification: Released under the GNU General Public License v3.0 only.
floss_license_osi_justification: GPL-3.0 is on the OSI-approved license list.
governance_justification: GOVERNANCE.md documents the maintainer role, decision-making, access continuity, succession plan, and co-maintainer process.
hardening_justification: SHA-pinned Docker base image (distroless), SHA-pinned all Actions, non-interactive git env vars, cosign-signed releases, SLSA v1.0 provenance, Dependabot alerts, weekly Scorecard + CodeQL + gosec + Dependency Review on every PR.
implement_secure_design_justification: Non-interactive git env everywhere (no cron-hang class); stdlib-only deps; SHA-pinned base image and Actions; keyless cosign; SLSA v1.0 attestations.
input_validation_justification: CLI flag parsing via stdlib flag; JSON API responses decoded into typed structs; unrecognised fields are ignored (forward-compatible).
installation_common_justification: GitHub Release archives per (OS, arch); Docker image at ghcr.io; `go install` also works.
know_common_errors_justification: gosec, CodeQL, and Dependency Review run on every PR via the shared security pipeline.
know_secure_design_justification: The maintainer has hardened the release path from day one: SLSA provenance, cosign keyless signing, SHA-pinned base images and Actions.
license_location_justification: Full LICENSE file at repository root; SPDX identifier declared in .goreleaser.yaml and Docker labels.
license_per_file_justification: Every .go source file carries `// SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-3.0-only` matching the LICENSE at the repo root.
maintained_justification: Actively maintained by @sebastienrousseau. SECURITY.md commits to best-effort response within 7 days.
maintenance_or_update_justification: The active maintainer commits to maintaining the latest minor version. Historical versions receive security fixes on a best-effort basis (documented in SECURITY.md).
no_leaked_credentials_justification: GitHub Push Protection enabled on the repo. Zero secret-scanning alerts historically.
release_notes_justification: CHANGELOG.md follows Keep a Changelog; every release will have a dated section with Added/Changed/Fixed/Security groupings.
repo_distributed_justification: Git is a distributed VCS by design.
repo_interim_justification: Every commit on main is a distinct addressable SHA; PR branches are preserved in the merge history.
repo_public_justification: Public GitHub repository, readable without an account.
repo_track_justification: Git — a distributed VCS that tracks every change, its author, and the commit timestamp.
report_archive_justification: GitHub Issues preserves the full comment history of every issue.
report_process_justification: Public bug reports via GitHub Issues; security disclosures via the private channel documented in SECURITY.md.
report_tracker_justification: GitHub Issues — publicly searchable, filterable, archived.
require_2FA_justification: The maintainer's GitHub account enforces 2FA. GitHub's defaults require 2FA for any collaborator.
roles_responsibilities_justification: GOVERNANCE.md 'Roles' section documents Maintainer and Contributor roles and each role's responsibilities.
secure_2FA_justification: Maintainer's 2FA uses a hardware security key (WebAuthn) as the primary factor.
signed_releases_justification: Every release artefact carries a signed SLSA v1.0 provenance attestation and the Docker image is signed by cosign in keyless OIDC mode.
sites_https_justification: Project URL, VCS URL, and download URL are all HTTPS.
small_tasks_justification: No 'good first issue' or 'help wanted' labelled issues yet. Artefact of the solo-maintainer model at initial import.
static_analysis_justification: CodeQL runs on every PR via the shared security pipeline. gosec runs as part of the same pipeline.
test_justification: Unit tests are a planned follow-up. Initial import verified via manual dry-run against 178 real repos.
two_person_review_justification: Structural blocker at Gold: single-maintainer project. Not falsely Met.
unittest_justification: Follow-up. Priority 1 after the first cut is proven in production.
unittest_policy_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md Pull Request Checklist requires make test to pass; test coverage is a future improvement tracked in CHANGELOG.
vulnerability_report_credit_justificatio: SECURITY.md commits to crediting vulnerability reporters (unless anonymity requested).
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: Private disclosure via GitHub Security Advisories.
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: SECURITY.md documents the private channel, 48-hour initial response, and 90-day coordinated disclosure timeline.
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: SECURITY.md commits to a best-effort initial response within 48 hours.
warnings_justification: `go vet ./...` runs on every PR via the shared Go CI pipeline.
